title: Ash Devices
summary: An engineering manifesto turned field guide: model, simulate, and operate cyber-physical systems as declarative Ash resources — where hardware is just an adapter and the simulator is the default runtime.
note: The request arrived as the long 'AshDevices Engineering Guide for Coding Agents', which BookBank split into ~250 fragments (one per heading, bullet, and arrow). Rather than a page per fragment, this build reorganizes the whole manifesto into 11 coherent chapters in Feynman's voice — keeping every idea and adding the researched use cases the guide explicitly asked for.

images.prompt: A serene, modern editorial illustration in flat vector style: on the right, a real industrial robot arm rendered in warm amber; on the left, its identical 'digital twin' rendered as a cool petrol-teal wireframe hologram, the two mirroring each other across a thin glowing seam labelled like a circuit trace. Beneath them a faint engineering blueprint grid. Cool slate background, petrol-teal + industrial-amber palette with a small live-green status dot, generous whitespace, soft depth, no text baked in. The mood: hardware made to feel like software.
images.alt: A physical robot arm mirrored by a glowing wireframe digital twin, wired through a thin adapter layer
images.caption: AshDevices: a real machine on one side, its declarative twin on the other, a thin adapter between.

images.prompt: A sweeping, optimistic editorial illustration in flat vector style: a 'self-driving laboratory' at night — robotic arms, an incubator, a microscope, and liquid handlers arranged on a bench, each device haloed by a cool petrol-teal wireframe 'digital twin' and connected by thin glowing signal-lines to a central orchestrator. A few live-green status dots. Industrial-amber hardware, petrol-teal twins, on a cool slate background with a faint blueprint grid. Generous whitespace, hopeful and futuristic, no text baked in.
images.alt: A self-driving laboratory: robotic arms, incubators and microscopes orchestrated as glowing resource nodes
images.caption: One control plane, many machines: the lab as a fleet of simulatable resources.
